examples: First employed by the ancient Greeks, trompe l'oeil was also popular with Roman muralists.
The show is not all trompe l’oeil: there are a handful of old master paintings too.
Other trompe l'oeil designs include an elegant garden vista, yellow columns and even a meticulously sketched blackbird .
There are Crosshatches, tales of transitions between realities that are separate but connected (see Trompe-l'Oeil, Portals).
The Tunisian cars and the Egyptian trompe l'oeil are unlikely to be acquired by the reigning monarchs.
